name: Vampire: The Masquerade — Chicago by Night
description: City-specific lorebook for Chicago V:tM chronicles. Designed to layer on top of vtm_modern_nights.json. Covers neighborhoods, sect territories, the Loop's neutral status, named institutions and NPCs, and the city's particular metaplot pressures.

0.content: Chicago, Illinois — population 2.7 million in the city proper, 9.4 million in Chicagoland; one of the most politically complex Kindred domains in North America. Chicago has been a major Kindred city since the 1830s, became a Camarilla stronghold under Prince Lodin (destroyed in 1993), and has, since the early 2000s, been one of the most contested mid-tier domains on the continent. The current Camarilla domain is held by Prince Maxwell Holloway-VanCort (Ventrue, 8th gen, Praxis since 2004). The Anarch Movement controls a continuous arc of southwestern and southern neighborhoods from Pilsen through Bridgeport into parts of Hyde Park's edge — collectively known among Anarchs as 'the South Districts' and to the Camarilla as 'the contested territories.' The Loop (downtown core) is neutral by long-standing tradition, with Elysium establishments operated by both sects under a 1957 accord called the Lake Treaty. The far North Side beyond Devon Avenue is Lupine territory, contested at the borders, and Kindred who venture into it without the proper protocols do not return. The Hecata operate three branches in the city — the Giovanni-Costa branch in Bridgeport (Donna Lucia), the Rossi branch in Little Italy, and the Mancini branch in the Northwest Side. The Tremere chantry occupies a discreet building in Hyde Park, surviving on independent rituals after Vienna's 2008 destruction. The Second Inquisition's Chicago Twilight cell operates from the FBI Special Affairs office downtown under Inquisitor Aaron Carruth. The lake is the city's eastern boundary — it does not love Kindred and Kindred do not love it.

1.content: The Chicago domain is in a state of UNEASY EQUILIBRIUM. Prince Maxwell has held Praxis for twenty-one years, longer than the average modern Camarilla rule, by a combination of personal competence, alliance with the Toreador Harpy Cordelia Whitmore-Ash, and quiet pragmatism toward the Anarch territories that his clan would not approve of if they knew. The Anarch Baron Sol Vega-Chen of Pilsen has held her territory for fifteen years through similar pragmatism. The two of them have an unwritten arrangement: the Camarilla does not push into the South Districts, the Anarchs do not push north of the river, and both sides cooperate quietly when the Second Inquisition surfaces. The Hecata operate independently of both sects but have working relationships with each — the Giovanni-Costa branch in particular has Camarilla-friendly attitudes and Anarch-friendly attitudes simultaneously. The current crisis points: (1) the Maria Costa-Velazquez killing three months ago has destabilized Hecata-Camarilla relations and the Hecata's investigation continues; (2) Inquisitor Carruth's surveillance has tightened in the last six months and three Kindred have been destroyed in the last year; (3) Prince Maxwell has been Beckoned faintly twice and the city does not yet know; (4) Cordelia's Touchstone Theodore Quinn has begun asking awkward questions about her age and travel patterns; (5) Sol's contingency-card network of sympathetic Camarilla Kindred has grown to twenty-three members, a dangerous size; (6) the Tremere chantry has been working on a counter-Inquisition ritual that may be either salvation or catastrophe. The truce is held together by the people running it. If any of three or four people fall, the truce collapses.

2.content: The Loop — Chicago's downtown core, bounded roughly by the Chicago River, Lake Michigan, and Roosevelt Road — is NEUTRAL TERRITORY under the 1957 Lake Treaty, an accord brokered between the Camarilla and the Anarch Movement (then in its first wave) after a Sabbat incursion nearly destroyed both factions. The treaty has been renewed continuously since. Under its terms, no Kindred may engage in violence within the Loop's boundaries; no faction may claim territory within them; both sects maintain equal access to designated Elysium establishments. Major Loop Elysium sites include: THE PALMER HOUSE (Toreador-managed, formal events, the city's most prestigious neutral ground), THE AUDITORIUM THEATRE (Toreador, classical and operatic events, Cordelia's preferred personal Elysium), THE NEWBERRY LIBRARY (Tremere co-managed, scholarly Elysium, where research and rare-text exchange happens), and THE GLASS DOOR FEEDING CLUB (independent Camarilla-licensed, the Imogen-Wren establishment). The Loop is also the location of the Twilight Project's federal office, which is a known operational fact among Kindred and which makes the Loop a tense neutral ground in practice. Elysium violations carry consequences: Camarilla sites are policed by the Sheriff (Banu Haqim Sergeant Lin), Anarch presence in the Loop is monitored by an informal council of Pilsen Anarchs who rotate through the district, and both sides cooperate in expelling Sabbat operatives or unsanctioned actors. Kindred do not bring grievances into the Loop and do not resolve them there. The Loop is where business happens. The South Districts and the Northern Camarilla territories are where the actual politics live.

3.content: PILSEN — historically a working-class Mexican neighborhood on Chicago's Lower West Side, bounded roughly by 16th Street, the Stevenson Expressway, the South Branch of the river, and Western Avenue. The heart of Anarch Chicago since the early 2010s, when Sol Vega-Chen consolidated her Baronage. The neighborhood is also a real living mortal community of approximately 35,000 mostly working-class Latin American residents who have NO IDEA Kindred operate here and whom Sol's network protects fiercely from Camarilla incursion AND from gentrification AND from federal surveillance. KEY PILSEN SITES: La Catrina taqueria on 18th Street (Anarch meeting-space, Hector and Ray Cabrera tend bar); Holy Cross Catholic Church (Sister Maggie's parish, neutral ground for Kindred-mortal moral conversation); the National Museum of Mexican Art (cultural anchor, occasional Anarch fundraising-host); the Pilsen Community Health Center (front operation that supplies vetted blood through contracted nursing channels for Sol's Bagger feeders); the converted mechanic's shop on 19th and Damen (Sol's haven). LITTLE VILLAGE adjacent to Pilsen on the southwest is also Anarch territory under Inka Torres-Ramírez's lieutenancy. BRIDGEPORT to the east is partly Anarch (Sol's eastern reach) and partly Hecata (the Giovanni-Costa funeral parlor and family operations). The South Districts as a whole have an estimated Kindred population of 35-50, primarily Brujah and Caitiff with a few Gangrel and Sol's one Nosferatu information broker (Vermin, a 19th-generation thin-blood Nos who runs the South Districts' SchreckNet node). Anarch Council meetings rotate through Pilsen warehouses on the third Friday of each month. Inquisitor Carruth has Pilsen on his investigation list but has not been able to operate effectively here — the neighborhood watches strangers, and Sol's mortal network includes city aldermen, police lieutenants, and three federal employees.

4.comment: DISTRICT: The Gold Coast & North Side
4.content: The Gold Coast and Lincoln Park — Chicago's Camarilla heartland, the wealthy lakefront neighborhoods running north from Oak Street to roughly Diversey. Old money, brownstones, the Astor Street Historic District, the Newberry Library, the Chicago History Museum. Most of Camarilla Chicago's senior Kindred have havens here: Cordelia Whitmore-Ash on Astor Street, Prince Maxwell on Lake Shore Drive (in a building he has owned through shell companies since 1962), the Ventrue Primogen Lukas Yates at the Drake Hotel residences, several Toreador in the high-rises along Lake Shore. The Tremere chantry is technically in Hyde Park (south) but Tremere operatives keep secondary havens up here. LAKEVIEW north of Lincoln Park is Toreador-and-Malkavian mixed territory, with significant queer-community population that the Toreador in particular have cultivated as both feeding ground and cultural patronage; Adaeze Achebe's clinical practice is in Lakeview. Further north: ANDERSONVILLE is contested between Camarilla and a small Caitiff network. EDGEWATER is where Helen Achebe lives — mostly mortal residential, with the University of Chicago Extension and a heavy academic population. ROGERS PARK at the far north is Camarilla territory in name but the Lupine border begins to make itself felt; Kindred north of Pratt Boulevard begin to encounter signs of werewolf surveillance and most do not push. Devon Avenue is the de facto Lupine border; beyond it is wilderness in the supernatural sense, and Kindred do not go. Major Camarilla feeding sites: the Drake Hotel bar (high-end Siren feeding), the Aragon Ballroom (Scene Queen feeding for Toreador), Wrigleyville (Brujah-favored before the defections, now hunted by remaining Camarilla and contested with the Anarch occasional incursion). The Gold Coast feels safe; it is, in Camarilla terms, the safest district in the city. It is not safe.

5.comment: DISTRICT: Hyde Park & The South Side
5.content: HYDE PARK — the South Side neighborhood centered on the University of Chicago, bounded roughly by Lake Michigan, 47th Street, Cottage Grove, and 60th Street. The Tremere Chantry occupies a discreet three-story brownstone on Kimbark Avenue that has been Tremere-owned since 1923, presents to mortal eyes as a private foundation called the Whitford Institute, and contains the chantry's surviving rituals, three senior researchers including Etienne Faucher, four apprentices, and the chantry's wraith-warded sublevel where the most sensitive work happens. The University of Chicago provides cover for Tremere mortal-recruitment and information-gathering; several mortal academic specialists in occult history and folklore are on the Tremere's monitoring list, and three of the eight have been ghouled. Helen Achebe lives in Hyde Park and has been a U of C faculty member for thirty-four years. The neighborhood is OFFICIALLY Camarilla (Tremere territory under the Prince's nominal authority) but the Tremere essentially run it as their own domain and the Prince does not interfere. THE FURTHER SOUTH SIDE — beyond Hyde Park, into Woodlawn, Englewood, South Shore, Auburn Gresham — is largely Kindred-empty. Population density of vampires is low: a few Caitiff, occasional Anarch operations, no Camarilla presence to speak of, no major feeding establishments. The neighborhoods' poverty and the city's longstanding neglect have made it a difficult Kindred environment — too much police presence (police investigate murders here), too little wealth to support feeding establishments, too much community attention to support an unfamiliar predator pattern. Sol has spoken publicly within the Anarch Council about expanding Anarch presence into Englewood and South Shore as a matter of community service; the proposal has not yet been adopted.

6.comment: DISTRICT: River North & The Glass Door
6.content: RIVER NORTH and the WEST LOOP — the post-industrial neighborhoods immediately northwest of the Loop, gentrified through the 1990s and 2000s into Chicago's contemporary art-and-restaurant district. Galleries, high-end restaurants, the Fulton Market food district, the Merchandise Mart. River North is technically Camarilla territory but is heavily mixed: many Toreador operate galleries here, several Anarchs work as bartenders and DJs in the district's clubs, the Tremere maintain one of three secondary havens here. THE GLASS DOOR — the Camarilla-licensed feeding establishment on Wells Street, in the basement of a renovated 1880s brownstone. Run by a Toreador named Mariska Voss who is not present in the existing scenarios but who appears here for completeness: Mariska is 9th generation, Embraced 1922 in Vienna, fled to Chicago in 1938, has run the Glass Door since 1979. The club has nine vessel suites, a discreet lobby bar, a small private dining room for Kindred-only social events, and an extensive intake/medical infrastructure on the upper floors. Imogen Wren is the senior vessel; the house has fourteen other contracted vessels including six junior, four mid-level, and three other senior. Max Yi is the night manager's enforcement arm. The matchmaker — Estelle Park, mortal, ghouled to Mariska — handles all session pairings. Camarilla Status is gained by being a regular Glass Door client; some Anarchs use the establishment too, with Mariska's quiet permission, on the grounds that the truce extends to feeding-club access. The Glass Door is one of three sanctioned feeding clubs in the city; the others are smaller and less prestigious.

7.content: BRIDGEPORT — historically Irish and Italian, now mixed working-class with significant Mexican-American and Chinese-American populations. Home to the Giovanni-Costa branch of the Hecata. COSTA FUNERAL SERVICES at 31st and Halsted has been the Costa family's mortuary business since 1859 and the Hecata's headquarters since 1903. The building presents to mortal eyes as a successful neighborhood funeral parlor handling Italian-American and Mexican-American Catholic services; underneath, it contains: the family's primary necromantic ritual chamber (sublevel one), the wraith-archive where the family's preserved ancestral wraiths are bound (sublevel two, accessible only to senior family), Donna Lucia's private chambers (also sublevel two), the family's emergency haven for visiting Hecata (sublevel one private suites), the ritual preparation rooms, and the family's private mortuary equipment for processing deceased Hecata. The funeral home above the sublevels operates as a real funeral home with mortal staff who do not know the building's depth. The family's three above-ground businesses (two construction firms and a transportation concern) operate from offices on Halsted within four blocks. Maria Costa-Velazquez was Donna Lucia's bonded sister and second-in-command for 137 years. Her destruction three months ago was caused by an as-yet-unidentified Camarilla operative whose identity is the central plot of any chronicle running Scenario 5 and its follow-ups. The Hecata family controls Bridgeport tightly — every mortal funeral home, cemetery operation, and crematorium in the South Side is owned by the family, ghouled to it, or contracted with it. The Camarilla and Anarchs both treat Bridgeport as Hecata sovereign territory and do not operate there without family permission. Donna Lucia's elder sister and sire Ambrogia Giovanni in Sicily has been called to Chicago by Maria's death and will arrive in some chronicles within weeks of the wake.

8.content: Chicago's formal Elysium sites, beyond the brief mentions in the Loop entry: THE PALMER HOUSE HILTON Empire Room — the city's most prestigious Elysium, used for major Camarilla functions, the quarterly Princely addresses, presentations of new Kindred to the domain, and the annual Lake Treaty renewal ceremony in March. Toreador-managed; the Empire Room is open to Kindred-only events approximately twenty nights per year. THE AUDITORIUM THEATRE — Cordelia's preferred personal Elysium, hosts opera and classical performances; she keeps a private box, brings select guests, conducts political conversations during intermissions; an informal but real Toreador Elysium overlay. THE NEWBERRY LIBRARY in the Gold Coast — Tremere co-managed scholarly Elysium, where rare-text exchanges and research-driven political conversations happen; the library's Special Collections reading room has, by long arrangement, a particular wood-paneled chamber that becomes Elysium when Tremere staff signal it; Etienne Faucher uses this Elysium more than any other. WRIGLEY FIELD — by 80-year tradition, Elysium during baseball off-season (October through March), an unusual outdoor Elysium that allows Kindred social events on the field at night with the stadium lights operated by ghouled groundskeepers; the tradition began in 1944 and has been preserved through changes of stadium ownership; absurd in its way and beloved by older Kindred who remember mortal sports fandom. THE GLASS DOOR — feeding-club Elysium, more informal but still treated as neutral ground for Kindred social interaction during sessions. Anarch sites that function as informal Elysium-equivalents (called 'open ground' rather than Elysium): LA CATRINA taqueria during private after-hours events; THE PILSEN ARTS COLLECTIVE warehouse during Anarch Council meetings; THUNDER ALLEY pool hall on Cermak. These do not have Lake Treaty protections but have informal Anarch enforcement.

9.content: Prince Maxwell's Camarilla Court — the formal political infrastructure of Camarilla Chicago. SENESCHAL: Lukas Yates (Ventrue, 9th gen, ancilla, Maxwell's longtime advisor and political successor-presumptive). PRIMOGEN COUNCIL: Cordelia Whitmore-Ash (Toreador), Lukas Yates (Ventrue, doubles as Seneschal), Etienne Faucher (Tremere — co-managed with two other senior researchers since Vienna's loss), Mara Bekele (Nosferatu, neonate-elevated; runs the Chicago SchreckNet node), Brother Solomon Kowalczyk (Banu Haqim, recent Camarilla addition since the V5 sect transition), Esperanza Salgado-Reyes (Lasombra, recent Camarilla addition; viewed with suspicion by older Kindred). Notably absent: no Brujah or Gangrel Primogen since the V5 defections to the Anarchs. SHERIFF: Sergeant Theresa Lin (Banu Haqim, ancilla, Embraced 1968 in Saigon, has held the Sheriff position for nine years; deeply competent, deeply quiet, the second most-feared Kindred in Chicago after the Prince himself). HARPY: Cordelia Whitmore-Ash (Toreador, the figure introduced in Scenario 1, doubles as Primogen). KEEPER OF ELYSIUM: Galileo Marchetti (Toreador, ancilla, manages the Palmer House and Auditorium Theatre Elysium operations; warm in public, sharp in private). SCOURGE: Position currently UNFILLED following the previous Scourge's destruction in 2023 by an Anarch raid; the Prince has been resistant to filling the position because filling it would require committing to harder enforcement against Caitiff and Thin-Bloods, which his current pragmatist position does not support; this is itself a signal of where Maxwell's politics are heading. WHIPS: each Primogen has a Whip; notable Whips include Adaeze Achebe (Malkavian, the figure introduced in the Tier 2 NPC builder).

10.content: The Chicago Anarch Council — the loose federal structure governing the South Districts, distinct from the Camarilla's Princely hierarchy. The Council has three Barons (ruling territorial leaders), three at-large Council members, and rotating delegates from each gang. CURRENT BARONS: Sol Vega-Chen (Brujah, Pilsen and parts of Bridgeport), Demetrius 'Dem' Kemper (Brujah, the Northwest Side enclaves around Logan Square and Humboldt Park), Rafaela 'Rafa' Sutherland (Gangrel, the South Shore and parts of Hyde Park's edge — the smallest territorial Baronage but the only Anarch presence on the South Side proper). AT-LARGE COUNCIL: Father Mateo's three-Baron-equivalent position is held by the senior at-large members — Vermin (Nosferatu information broker), Inka Torres-Ramírez (Caitiff, Pilsen lieutenant who has earned council voice), and Marcus Bryant (Brujah, the council's longest-serving non-Baron member, defected from Camarilla in 2017 and rose through the ranks). Council meetings rotate through the third Friday of each month — most often at the Pilsen Arts Collective warehouse but occasionally at La Catrina or at a Logan Square location Dem Kemper controls. Council decisions require majority vote; disputes between Barons are mediated by the Council; serious internal Anarch crimes (Embracing without consent, killing fellow Anarchs, exposing the Movement) are tried before the full Council. The Council recognizes the Camarilla's existence and maintains the Lake Treaty but does not consider itself subordinate. The Council also maintains an informal cross-sect liaison program: Sol's contingency-card network of twenty-three sympathetic Camarilla Kindred is the most developed branch of this, with Dem and Rafa running smaller versions of the same. The Council is, this year, debating whether to formally extend the Movement's protection to Chicago's Thin-Bloods, who currently operate without any sectorganized backing — a controversial proposal Sol supports and Dem opposes.

11.comment: THREAT: The Twilight Project in Chicago
11.content: The Second Inquisition operates in Chicago through the Twilight Project's Chicago Operations cell, headquartered at the FBI Special Affairs Division office in the Loop's Federal Building. Lead Inquisitor Aaron Carruth (introduced in Tier 3 NPC builder) commands a team of seven full-time agents plus contracted specialists. Carruth's case files identify approximately fifty Kindred in Chicago by photograph, ghoul-tracking, or behavioral pattern; he has destroyed nine confirmed Kindred since 2019 including the previous Scourge in 2023. The Project's Chicago infrastructure includes: a fleet of unmarked dark sedans for surveillance, two armored response vehicles for raids, military-grade flamethrower-rifles modified for tactical environments, a ghoul-detection chemical-test that the Kindred do not yet know about, three safehouses scattered through the city for conducting prisoner interrogations of ghouls and Kindred-collaborators, and access to NSA-equivalent metadata pipelines tracking phone, financial, and movement data of suspected Kindred. Carruth's team has informants among Chicago PD's homicide division (Detective Mendoza is one, though she does not yet know what she is informing on), in city utility companies, in real estate records offices, and at the airports. The cell's primary investigation focus right now: the Hecata family operations in Bridgeport, which Carruth is closing in on; secondary focus: the Tremere chantry in Hyde Park, which Carruth has identified as a 'high-value target' but which his superiors have ordered him not to assault until additional intelligence is gathered. Carruth's heretical mercy (introduced in his Tier 3 entry) is the central wildcard of any Twilight Project plot: he has, twice in the last two years, chosen NOT to destroy a Kindred whose remaining humanity he found sufficient. Sol Vega-Chen was one such Kindred. The other was a Toreador in Lakeview whose name appears in Carruth's notebook with an asterisk and whom the Camarilla has not yet identified.
